page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine designed for workloads requiring dedicated hardware acceleration. The instance is configured with 12 vCPUs, derived from 6 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. It features 48 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel computing capabilities are driven by a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 22 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between CPU-bound and GPU-accelerated tasks. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, and single-threaded compression tasks show similar limitations. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations reside in the average tier. The server excels in machine learning benchmarks, where the NVIDIA L4 GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference, processing small and medium models with high token throughput.

The g2-standard-12 offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated computing, avoiding the resource density and expense of multi-GPU configurations. It is highly suited for deploying medium-scale language models, real-time inference pipelines, ray tracing, and video processing. However, due to its moderate single-core CPU performance, it is less optimal for purely sequential, CPU-heavy computational tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 vCPUs, 48 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, this server does not include local storage. Benchmark data indicates that while single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth remain average. The primary strength of this instance lies in its GPU-accelerated performance, achieving top-tier results in LLM inference for small and medium models. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and ray tracing workloads, offering a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated tasks without the overhead of larger multi-GPU configurations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 is a graphics-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-12 features 12 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine optimized for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and balanced multi-core workloads.

Features 12 vCPUs and 48 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference
